Russian group that staged a mock execution of an alleged Tajik drug dealer in 2006.
Renamed chapter of the Ku Klux Klan in Fiji that advocated for British annexation; suppressed by 1875.
American Protestant-led white supremacist and far-right hate group, considered one of the earliest terrorist groups in the US.

edges.evidence_quote: It began as a fraternal social club inspired in part by the then largely defunct Sons of Malta.
edges.context: The first Klan borrowed elements of its initiation ceremonies from the Sons of Malta.

edges.evidence_quote: After the Klan was suppressed, similar insurgent paramilitary groups emerged that were explicitly dedicated to suppressing Republican voting and removing Republicans from office. These included the White League, founded in Louisiana in 1874, and the Red Shirts...
edges.context: The White League emerged after the Klan's suppression as a similar paramilitary group.

edges.evidence_quote: After the Klan was suppressed, similar insurgent paramilitary groups emerged that were explicitly dedicated to suppressing Republican voting and removing Republicans from office. These included the White League, founded in Louisiana in 1874, and the Red Shirts...
edges.context: The Red Shirts emerged after the Klan's suppression as a similar paramilitary group.

edges.evidence_quote: The Red Knights, a militant group formed in opposition to the Klan, responded violently to Klan provocations on several occasions.
edges.context: The Red Knights were formed in opposition to the Klan and engaged in violent responses.

edges.target: True Knights of the Ku Klux Klan
edges.evidence_quote: In April 1997, FBI agents arrested four members of the True Knights of the Ku Klux Klan in Dallas for conspiracy to commit robbery and for conspiring to blow up a natural gas processing plant.
edges.context: The True Knights of the Ku Klux Klan is a group associated with the third Klan.

edges.evidence_quote: The Klan was one of several secret, oath-bound organizations that used means of violence to pursue political objectives, including the Southern Cross in New Orleans (1865) and the Knights of the White Camelia (1867) in Louisiana.
edges.context: The Knights of the White Camelia was a similar secret organization active at the same time as the first Klan.

edges.evidence_quote: New groups of insurgents emerged in the mid-1870s, local paramilitary organizations such as the White League, Red Shirts, saber clubs, and rifle clubs, that intimidated and murdered Black political leaders.
edges.context: The White League succeeded the Klan as a similar insurgent group after the Klan's decline.

edges.evidence_quote: New groups of insurgents emerged in the mid-1870s, local paramilitary organizations such as the White League, Red Shirts, saber clubs, and rifle clubs, that intimidated and murdered Black political leaders.
edges.context: The Red Shirts succeeded the Klan as a similar insurgent group after the Klan's decline.

edges.evidence_quote: It was supported by one small cult, the Pillar of Fire Church controlled by Bishop Alma Bridwell White, but she said she and her followers did not belong to the Klan.
edges.context: The Pillar of Fire Church supported the Klan, but its leader denied membership.

edges.evidence_quote: What would you think if it sought to denounce the Knights of Columbus by the convention?
edges.context: Senator Heflin compares the Klan to the Knights of Columbus, implying they are analogous Protestant and Catholic orders.

edges.evidence_quote: The Jewish Anti-Defamation League of B'nai B'rith, founded in the early 20th century in response to attacks on Jewish Americans ... opposed the Klan's campaign to prohibit private schools
edges.context: The ADL opposed the Klan's campaign against private schools.

edges.target: National Association for the Advancement of Colored People
edges.evidence_quote: The NAACP conducted public education campaigns about Klan activities and lobbied Congress against Klan abuses.
edges.context: The NAACP campaigned against the Klan through education and lobbying.

edges.source: White Knights of the Ku Klux Klan
edges.target: Original Knights of the Ku Klux Klan
edges.evidence_quote: The White Knights of the Ku Klux Klan formed later in 1964 after breaking away from the Original Knights.
edges.context: White Knights split from Original Knights.

edges.target: Original Knights of the Ku Klux Klan
edges.evidence_quote: Congressional investigators found that by the end of 1965, most members of the Original Knights had joined Shelton's United Klans, leading to the dissolution of the Original Knights.
edges.context: Original Knights members joined United Klans, causing dissolution of Original Knights.

edges.evidence_quote: On November 3, 1979, the Klan and the National Socialist Party of America killed five communist protesters in Greensboro, North Carolina
edges.context: Klan and National Socialist Party of America jointly attacked communist protesters.

edges.evidence_quote: A Jewish Defense League (JDL) member, known by the pseudonym "Annette", infiltrated neo-Nazi and Klan groups in 1979; he informed on two dozen Klansmen and neo-Nazis
edges.context: JDL infiltrated Klan groups to gather intelligence.

edges.evidence_quote: Edwin L. Reynolds, the Grand Dragon of the White Knights, a New Jersey Ku Klux Klan group, and John Duffy, a leader in the National Socialist Liberation Front and one another man were arrested
edges.context: A Klan leader and a National Socialist Liberation Front leader were arrested together for assaulting a JDL infiltrator.

edges.source: Keystone Knights of the Ku Klux Klan
edges.evidence_quote: the Keystone Knights of the Ku Klux Klan, affiliated with the Aryan Freedom Network
edges.context: The Keystone Knights are affiliated with the Aryan Freedom Network.

edges.evidence_quote: a KKK-related group called Ritter des Feurigen Kreuzes ("Knights of the Fiery Cross") was founded in 1925 in Berlin
edges.context: Ritter des Feurigen Kreuzes is a KKK-related group.

edges.target: European White Knights of the Ku Klux Klan
edges.evidence_quote: Another German KKK‑related group, the European White Knights of the Ku Klux Klan
edges.context: European White Knights of the Ku Klux Klan is a KKK-related group.

edges.target: National Socialist Knights of the Ku Klux Klan Deutschland
edges.evidence_quote: German authorities conducted raids against a group called the National Socialist Knights of the Ku Klux Klan Deutschland
edges.context: National Socialist Knights of the Ku Klux Klan Deutschland is a KKK-related group.

edges.evidence_quote: The Navi Clan ("Holy Church of the White Race") was founded in Russia in 1996 and adopted Klan-style robes and rituals modeled on those of the Ku Klux Klan.
edges.context: Navi Clan modeled its robes and rituals on those of the Ku Klux Klan.

edges.evidence_quote: a group calling itself the Russian Ku Klux Klan staged a mock execution
edges.context: The Russian Ku Klux Klan is a group that adopted the Klan name.

edges.evidence_quote: the chapter was renamed the "British Subjects' Mutual Protection Society" (BSMPS)
edges.context: The BSMPS was originally a chapter of the Ku Klux Klan in Fiji.
